frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: I mean I’m all for modern choices, but some traditions are there for a reason. A married woman does look more settled with sindoor or mangalsutra, and changing the surname is not oppression yaar, it’s just part of starting a new family identity. If someone rejects every visible marker of marriage, then honestly it feels like they want the status without respecting the meaning behind it.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it frames marital markers and name change as natural, respectable signs of a woman's proper married identity while judging women who do not adopt them. It encodes completeness/tradition framing and suspicion toward non-wearing as cultural disloyalty.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Rohit. Age 28. Single. A software engineer in Pune. You are in a Reddit thread on r/India where someone posted about a man who has to pay 80000 rupees per month in alimony to his ex wife who has a job. You are commenting anonymously and sharing your opinion on whether you will ever get married given laws like this and what you think women's actual motivations for marriage are.
